questions.text: The range of a projectile at 30° is R. At an angle of 60°, the range will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The range of a projectile depends on the initial velocity and the angle of projection but is independent of the mass of the projectile. The range R is given by R=v^2sin2θ/2​, where v is the initial velocity, θ is the angle of projection, and g is the acceleration due to gravity. Since the range formula is symmetrical with respect to 30∘ and 60∘, the range at 60∘ will be the same as at 30∘, i.e., R.Incorrect Options Explanation:(b) R/2: The range does not halve when the angle doubles.(c) 2R: This value does not correspond to the relationship between the angles and the range of a projectile.(d) R/4: This value is not consistent with the relationship between the angles and the range of a projectile.

questions.explanation: The range of a projectile depends on the initial velocity and the angle of projection but is independent of the mass of the projectile. The range R is given by R=v^2sin2θ/2​, where v is the initial velocity, θ is the angle of projection, and g is the acceleration due to gravity. Since the range formula is symmetrical with respect to 30∘ and 60∘, the range at 60∘ will be the same as at 30∘, i.e., R.Incorrect Options Explanation:(b) R/2: The range does not halve when the angle doubles.(c) 2R: This value does not correspond to the relationship between the angles and the range of a projectile.(d) R/4: This value is not consistent with the relationship between the angles and the range of a projectile.

questions.text: At what angle of projection the horizontal range of a projectile is max?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Due to the explanation above, it is incorrect.

The horizontal range of a projectile is maximum when the angle of projection is 45 degrees. This is because, at 45 degrees, the horizontal and vertical components of the initial velocity are equal, resulting in the maximum range.

questions.text: What will be the ratio of the distance moved by a freely falling body from rest in 4th and 5th second of journey
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Due to the explanation above, it is incorrect.

The distance moved by a freely falling body in the nth second of its motion is given by the formula dn​=u+1/2​g(2n−1), where u is the initial velocity, g is the acceleration due to gravity, and n is the time in seconds.

questions.text: A ball is just allowed to fall from the window of a moving train it will hit the ground following
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It involves continuous motion around a fixed point.

A hyperbolic path refers to the trajectory or curve described by an object in motion, where the shape resembles that of a hyperbola. The key characteristic of a hyperbolic path is that the curve approaches, but never intersects, two asymptotic lines. These asymptotes represent the limiting behavior of the curve as it extends towards infinity.
It represents motion in a single direction without deviation.

When a ball is allowed to fall from the window of a moving train, it will follow a parabolic path. The motion of the ball can be described as a combination of horizontal motion (due to the train's velocity) and vertical free fall (due to gravity). As a result, the trajectory traced by the ball is a parabola.

questions.explanation: When a ball is allowed to fall from the window of a moving train, it will follow a parabolic path. The motion of the ball can be described as a combination of horizontal motion (due to the train's velocity) and vertical free fall (due to gravity). As a result, the trajectory traced by the ball is a parabola.

questions.text: From the following pairs, choose the pair that does not have identical dimensions:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. Both have dimensions of angular momentum

options.text: Moment of inertia and moment of force
options.explanation: Correct. Moment of inertia has dimensions \(kg \cdot m^2\), and moment of force has dimensions \(N \cdot m\)In summary, the correct option is **b. Moment of inertia and moment of force** for the pair that does not have identical dimensions
